$comment: Machine-readable surface for a future /apply-pattern installer. Human spec: patterns/elevated-access-session/pattern.md. Expressed AS an invariant cluster (see docs/patterns-registry.md#patterns-as-clusters-of-invariants). Promoted from candidate to specified; realized 1:1 in dogeared-coach (invariant ids stamped in the guard's code comments).
title: Revocable Elevated-Access Session (support impersonation)
applies_when: multi-tenant product where support/ops must act AS a tenant user to reproduce issues
you need time-boxed, revocable, fully-audited operator act-as
there are actions impersonation must NEVER be able to take (destructive, auth-factor, operator plane)
components: signer-minted-session: token subject=target, imp claim {actor,target,session_id,iat} set server-side; un-forgeable at the signing root
independent-ttl: elevated window enforced against imp.iat, NOT the identity-provider token life
future-skew-guard: imp.iat more than a small tolerance ahead is rejected
fail-closed-revocation: revoked_at + a revocation lookup that denies when it cannot confirm the session is live
no-nested-impersonation: an impersonated session cannot mint another
boundary-checked-deny-list: blocked from /admin/*, auth-factor family (prefix-matched), and destructive/GDPR; rules exported for the banner
dual-identity-audit: every impersonated mutation writes an actor+target row BEFORE the mutation, fail-closed
fixed-middleware-order: resolve -> guard -> deny-list -> audit -> handler, asserted
non-dismissible-banner: the operator always sees they are acting as someone else

invariants.$comment: The cluster this pattern guarantees. Generic ids are the pattern's own; realized_as cites the provenance app's app-specific id + code. Note the app has minor doc/code id drift on future-skew (code comment tags it INV-ADM-009); code file:line is the authoritative provenance.

cluster.statement: TTL enforced against the session's own iat, independent of the IdP token life.
Revocable per-session; revocation lookup is fail-closed.
Future-skew guard: imp.iat more than tolerance ahead is rejected.
Deny-listed from operator plane + auth-factor + destructive/GDPR, boundary-checked prefix; rules exported for the banner.
Every impersonated write is dual-identity audited, fail-closed, before the mutation.
Un-forgeable at the signing root: minted by the trusted signer with an actor claim the caller cannot self-assert.

ttl.description: Server-enforced elevated window, measured from imp.iat.

future_skew_tolerance.description: Max tolerated forward clock drift on imp.iat.

signer.description: What mints the session token; behind provider-neutral-adapter.

deny_prefixes.description: Route families an impersonated session may never reach.

audit_sink.description: Where dual-identity audit rows are written; must fail closed.

metrics.desc: impersonated writes with no dual-identity audit row (target 0; gate-asserted)
requests accepted past TTL or after revocation (target 0)
impersonated requests that reached a denied family (target 0)

depends_on.provider-neutral-adapter: signer + identity provider sit behind the neutral seam
depends_on.multi-tenant-isolation: the /admin/* operator plane the session is denied from is that pattern's out-of-band admin plane

feeds.improvement-loop: the dual-identity audit stream is trusted-origin operational signal
